@ZhaohuiS 
This fix is good to have and we can proceed with committing this.
But this fix doesn't address the errors that we are seeing ... 

Here are some logs from local setup - 
```
From $ sudo sonic-db-cli COUNTERS_DB hgetall "COUNTERS_PORT_NAME_MAP”:
 'Ethernet512': 'oid:0x100000000001d', 'Ethernet513': 'oid:0x100000000003e

in orchagent:
#############
During per port capability check (via buffer overflow method):
##############################################################
2026 Apr  1 14:17:35.139146 moby206 NOTICE swss#orchagent: :- operator(): PORT_PHY_ATTR: Port Ethernet512 supports SAI_PORT_ATTR_RX_SNR attribute
2026 Apr  1 14:17:35.140446 moby206 NOTICE swss#orchagent: :- operator(): PORT_PHY_ATTR: Port Ethernet513 supports SAI_PORT_ATTR_RX_SNR attribute

in syncd:
########
During fetching lanes for PORT Ethernet512, and Ethernet513
###########################################################
2026 Apr  1 14:17:35.480211 moby206 NOTICE syncd#syncd: :- updatePortLaneCountMap: PORT_PHY_ATTR: m_portLaneCountMap[rid:0x100000076][170] = 1
2026 Apr  1 14:17:35.480480 moby206 NOTICE syncd#syncd: :- updatePortLaneCountMap: PORT_PHY_ATTR: m_portLaneCountMap[rid:0x100000076][149] = 1
2026 Apr  1 14:17:35.479682 moby206 NOTICE syncd#syncd: :- updatePortLaneCountMap: PORT_PHY_ATTR: m_portLaneCountMap[rid:0x1000000fe][170] = 1
2026 Apr  1 14:17:35.479941 moby206 NOTICE syncd#syncd: :- updatePortLaneCountMap: PORT_PHY_ATTR: m_portLaneCountMap[rid:0x1000000fe][149] = 1


After that we keep getting this error every time during collectData call for these two ports
############################################################################################
2026 Apr  1 14:26:26.325268 moby206 ERR syncd#syncd: [none] SAI_API_PORT:brcm_sai_get_port_attribute_cmn:11189 bcm_port_phy_control_get rx snr failed with error Feature unavailable (0xfffffff0).
2026 Apr  1 14:26:26.325268 moby206 ERR syncd#syncd: :- collectData: Failed to get port attr for VID 0x100000000001d, RID:0x100000076: -2
2026 Apr  1 14:26:26.485584 moby206 ERR syncd#syncd: [none] SAI_API_PORT:brcm_sai_get_port_attribute_cmn:11189 bcm_port_phy_control_get rx snr failed with error Feature unavailable (0xfffffff0).
2026 Apr  1 14:26:26.485584 moby206 ERR syncd#syncd: :- collectData: Failed to get port attr for VID 0x100000000003e, RID:0x1000000fe: -2
```

Above suggests that the Attribute init call returns properly.
We see this issue because the two mgmt 10g Ports were never to be installed for phy counters in the first place (this issue is being checked on by broadcom CSP : 12457122)
Meanwhile the log ignores in mgmt are sufficient to ignore the errors till bcm fixes this.

But, your fix seems correct and is needed anyway .. so LGTM please proceed with committing this.

The failing checks [Azure.sonic-sairedis (Test vstest)](https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-sairedis/pull/1815/checks?check_run_id=68963421962) and [Azure.sonic-sairedis (TestAsan vstest)](https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-sairedis/pull/1815/checks?check_run_id=68963429576)

This seems like an infra issue. This check already passed for the master branch.    
I already tried re-running these failed checks 3 times, without success.
Please help with this.

**Review: Two items to address**

1. **Merge conflict with PR #1788**: This PR's `VendorSai.cpp` diff includes the identical `HAVE_SAI_QUERY_STATS_ST_CAPABILITY` guard block from #1788. If both merge independently, there will be a git conflict. Suggest either combining them into one PR, or merging #1788 first and rebasing this one on top.

2. **Companion buildimage PR sonic-net/sonic-buildimage#26013 is missing `SAI_STATS_EXT_SWITCH_SUPPORTED=0`**: The `sai.profile` changes in #26013 only add `SAI_STATS_ST_CAPABILITY_SUPPORTED=0` (for the #1788 fix), but do not add `SAI_STATS_EXT_SWITCH_SUPPORTED=0` (needed by this PR). Without it, the `sai_get_stats_ext` crash on TH1 switch objects would still happen after all three PRs merge. Please update #26013 to include this key as well.

Thanks for the review @Gfrom2016!

**Item 1 — Merge conflict with #1788:**

This is intentional — #1789 is stacked on top of #1788's branch to avoid a cherry-pick conflict on \VendorSai.cpp\ (both PRs modify the same function). The plan is:
1. Merge #1788 into master first
2. Rebase this branch (\ ix/brcm-legacy-compat-master-issue2\) onto the updated master and force-push
3. The \HAVE_SAI_QUERY_STATS_ST_CAPABILITY\ block will then appear as pre-existing context in the diff, not as added lines — no conflict

Will rebase after #1788 lands.

**Item 2 — \SAI_STATS_EXT_SWITCH_SUPPORTED=0\ in buildimage:**

This key is present — it is in a separate companion PR [sonic-net/sonic-buildimage#26014](https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-buildimage/pull/26014), which is the buildimage counterpart specifically for this PR (#1789). [sonic-net/sonic-buildimage#26013](https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-buildimage/pull/26013) is the companion for PR #1788 (the \SAI_STATS_ST_CAPABILITY_SUPPORTED\ fix) and intentionally only contains that key.

Summary of PR pairing:
| sairedis PR | buildimage PR | sai.profile key |
|---|---|---|
| #1788 | sonic-buildimage#26013 | \SAI_STATS_ST_CAPABILITY_SUPPORTED=0\ |
| #1789 (this PR) | sonic-buildimage#26014 | \SAI_STATS_EXT_SWITCH_SUPPORTED=0\ |

I will update the PR description to make the buildimage companion link explicit.

@prgeor Please give revirew approval again. All checks are passing now.

@prgeor 

regarding PHY OID -> Port OID -> EthernetXX mapping?

`sonic-db-cli COUNTERS_DB hgetall COUNTERS_PORT_SERDES_ID_TO_PORT_ID_MAP`
Gives the PORT_SERDES_VID -> PORT_VID mapped pair.
After this we can we map how we have mapped port-vid with interface name.

`sonic-db-cli COUNTERS_DB hgetall COUNTERS_PORT_NAME_MAP`
Gives EthernetXX -> PORT_VID mapped pair.

@lguohan Fixed — the test failure was `aspellcheck.pl` (spellcheck), not a unit test failure. All 56 unit tests passed including our updated FlexCounter tests. Added 'usleep' to `tests/aspell.en.pws`. Force-pushed.
Azure Pipelines successfully started running 1 pipeline(s).

Addressed both Copilot review comments:

1. **Silent timeout → ADD_FAILURE()**: Both `waitForCounterKeys()` and `waitForCounterValue()` now call `ADD_FAILURE()` with diagnostics (expected vs actual keys, key+field) when the timeout expires, instead of silently returning.

2. **removeTimeStamp DB mutation in poll loop**: Extracted `countNonTimestampKeys()` helper that filters TIME_STAMP from the in-memory vector without deleting it from the DB. No more write-per-iteration race with the producer thread.

Force-pushed with both fixes.
Azure Pipelines successfully started running 1 pipeline(s).
Azure Pipelines successfully started running 1 pipeline(s).

Updated and ready for review. Summary of changes since last push:

- **`waitForCounterValue`** now takes an explicit `expectedValue` parameter (waits for exact match)
- Added **`waitForNonZeroCounterValue`** helper for tests with bulk init phases that write zeros before real data
- Fixed **`counterIdChange`** test: when switching between bulk/non-bulk modes, stale values persisted in Redis. Now waits for the specific expected value (e.g., `IF_IN_UCAST_PKTS` changing from "200" to "20") instead of just any non-empty value
- Fixed **`addRemoveCounter`**: uses `expectedValues[0]` when available, falls back to `waitForNonZeroCounterValue` for tests with custom verify functions (like bulkChunksize)
- Added `usleep` to `aspell.en.pws` dictionary

**Local test results: 91/92 pass** — the only failure is `AttrVersionChecker.isSufficientVersion` which is a pre-existing upstream issue.
All 12 FlexCounter tests pass (including bulkChunksize + counterIdChange).
aspellcheck + swsslogentercheck both pass.

sonic-vpp t1-lag sonic-mgmt test is in PR checker. You can trigger it like this PR: https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-buildimage/pull/25369

> Thanks for the contribution. This is an important feature. Can you clarify what tests you have run?

Hey, I have ran tests on a local containerlab topology: 
- 2x sonic-vpp routers and 2x linux PCs (praqma/network-multitool:latest image)
- bgp evpn peering between routers for vtep discovery and mac learning
- PCs attached to access VLANs (untagged) on each router

With this setup I tested and confirmed:
- vpp vxlan tunnel creation with 'vppctl show vxlan tunnel'
- bridge domain membership with tunnel interface 'vppctl show bridge-domain'
- verified sai objects in redis asic_db (tunnel, tunnel maps, tunnel map entries)
- single vni vlan mapping
Commenter does not have sufficient privileges for PR 1761 in repo sonic-net/sonic-sairedis

> sonic-vpp t1-lag sonic-mgmt test is in PR checker. You can trigger it like this PR: [sonic-net/sonic-buildimage#25369](https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-buildimage/pull/25369)

Can you run sonic-vpp sonic-mgmt to make sure it doesn't break anything?

> > sonic-vpp t1-lag sonic-mgmt test is in PR checker. You can trigger it like this PR: [sonic-net/sonic-buildimage#25369](https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-buildimage/pull/25369)
> 
> Can you run sonic-vpp sonic-mgmt to make sure it doesn't break anything?

nvm. You already did :)

> any test cases?

It seems there is no EVPN test case in sonic-mgmt. sonic-vpp doesn't have a UT infrastructure either. Max from Arista and his team is working on that.
